industries.name: Petroleum Refining Catalysts (FCC)
uses: Lanthanum oxide (La₂O₃) stabilizes the zeolite Y catalyst used in fluid catalytic cracking (FCC) — FCC converts heavy gas oil to gasoline, diesel, and petrochemical feedstocks. Lanthanum exchanged into the zeolite prevents dealumination and maintains catalytic activity at 700°C+

Rare earth-exchanged Y zeolite (REY, REUSY) containing lanthanum and cerium is the workhorse of the global petroleum refining industry — virtually every oil refinery worldwide uses FCC catalysts containing rare earth elements
Lanthanum in FCC catalysts improves gasoline yield, octane number, and catalyst stability under repeated cracking and regeneration cycles at extreme temperatures

Lanthanum aluminate (LaAlO₃) as catalyst support for automotive exhaust three-way catalysts — improves thermal stability of the alumina washcoat at the high temperatures of close-coupled catalyst positions

companies.name: BASF Catalysts
companies.use: Major FCC catalyst manufacturer. Produces lanthanum-stabilized zeolite Y FCC catalysts for petroleum refineries globally. Lanthanum content in FCC catalysts is a significant end use — BASF processes tonnes of lanthanum oxide annually for catalyst production.

companies.name: Grace (W.R. Grace)
companies.use: World's largest FCC catalyst producer. Manufactures Davison-brand rare earth-exchanged zeolite FCC catalysts supplied to refineries on every continent. Lanthanum and cerium are incorporated into every tonne of catalyst produced.

industries.name: Optical Glass & Camera Lenses
uses: Lanthanum oxide (La₂O₃, 20–50% by weight) in optical glass formulations: increases refractive index (nd up to 1.90) while maintaining low dispersion (Abbe number Vd 40–50) — enabling compact, aberration-corrected lens designs impossible with conventional silicate glass
La-dense optical glass used in camera zoom lenses, microscope objectives, and telescope eyepieces for high-resolution, low-chromatic-aberration optics in demanding imaging applications
Lanthanum fluoride (LaF₃) in optical coatings and in specialty glass for UV-transmitting optics
Lanthanum aluminate (LAO) as substrate material for growing high-temperature superconductor thin films and complex oxide epitaxial layers in research and quantum computing applications

companies.name: Schott AG
companies.use: Major specialty optical glass manufacturer producing lanthanum-dense glasses (LaK, LaSF series) for precision optics, camera lenses, and scientific instruments. Lanthanum content of high-index glass formulations is substantial per tonne of glass produced.

companies.name: Canon
companies.use: Major camera lens manufacturer consuming lanthanum optical glass in its UD (ultra-low dispersion) and LD elements for telephoto and zoom lenses. Canon L-series lenses use lanthanum-containing glass to achieve high refractive index with low dispersion.

industries.name: Nickel-Metal Hydride Batteries
uses: Lanthanum-rich misch metal (La-Ce-Pr-Nd alloy) is the major component of the AB₅-type hydrogen storage alloy (LaNi₅ and mischmetal Ni₅) used as the negative electrode in nickel-metal hydride (NiMH) batteries

Hybrid electric vehicle (HEV) NiMH battery packs: Toyota Prius (all generations), Honda Insight, and most hybrid vehicles use NiMH batteries containing lanthanum — each Prius battery pack contains ~10–15 kg of rare earth elements including significant lanthanum
Industrial NiMH batteries for power tools, medical equipment, and portable electronics still use lanthanum-based metal hydride anodes in applications where NiMH is preferred over lithium-ion
Each Toyota Prius sold represents ~10kg of rare earth-containing metal hydride material — Toyota has sold 6+ million Prius globally, representing a significant cumulative lanthanum demand

companies.name: Toyota Motor Corporation
companies.use: World's largest hybrid vehicle manufacturer and major NiMH battery consumer. Prius and other Toyota hybrids use NiMH batteries containing lanthanum-based metal hydride anodes. Has significant interest in rare earth supply security for battery production.

companies.name: Primearth EV Energy (Toyota/Panasonic JV)
companies.use: Joint venture producing NiMH battery packs for Toyota, Honda, and Ford hybrid vehicles. Major consumer of lanthanum-containing AB₅ metal hydride alloy for battery negative electrodes.

industries.name: Specialty & Emerging Applications
uses: Lanthanum nickel oxide (LaNiO₃) and lanthanum manganite (LaMnO₃) perovskite electrodes for solid oxide fuel cells (SOFCs) — lanthanum-based perovskites are the standard cathode material for SOFC oxygen reduction
Lanthanum hexaboride (LaB₆) electron emitter: thermionic cathode material for electron microscopes (SEM, TEM) and electron beam lithography systems — 10× higher brightness than tungsten filaments

Lanthanum strontium manganite (LSM) and lanthanum strontium cobalt ferrite (LSCF) perovskite cathodes in SOFC stacks — the primary cathode material in commercial SOFC systems from Bloom Energy and other manufacturers
La₂O₃ as gate dielectric in experimental high-k transistor structures — high dielectric constant (εr ~27) and bandgap make lanthanum oxide a candidate for next-generation CMOS gate insulators

companies.name: JEOL
companies.use: Major electron microscope manufacturer using lanthanum hexaboride (LaB₆) cathodes in its SEM and TEM instruments. LaB₆ emitters provide higher brightness and longer lifetime than tungsten filaments for high-resolution imaging.

companies.name: Bloom Energy
companies.use: Leading SOFC manufacturer. Uses lanthanum strontium manganite (LSM) and lanthanum strontium cobalt ferrite (LSCF) perovskite cathode materials in its Energy Server solid oxide fuel cell stacks for distributed power generation.
